Something that can not be imitated.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(a)
Inimitable means incapable of being duplicated. The word duplicity means dishonest behavior that is intended to trick someone. Something that is impossible to explain is called inexplicable and something that is impossible to avoid or prevent is termed as inevitable.

Q12 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Let A be a 3 × 3 matrix with rank 2. Then AX = 0 has

Correct answer: A, B
Solution

(a, b)
If r is the rank of matrix A and n × n is the order of matrix then we shall have (n – r) linearly independent non-trivial solutions. Any linear combination of these (n – r) solutions will also be a solution of AX = 0.

Q17 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

In the context of decision trees, the Gini index is preferred over entropy when:
S1 : The dataset is large and efficiency is a concern.
S2 : The focus is on maximizing information gain.
S3 : There are many categorical features with high cardinality.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The Gini index is often preferred over entropy for efficiency reasons, especially with large datasets. Entropy focuses on maximizing information gain, which is not directly related to the choice of the Gini index.

Q18 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Systematic sampling requires a randomly chosen starting point and a fixed periodic interval for selecting subsequent samples.
S2 : Systematic sampling can lead to biased results if the population has a hidden pattern related to the sampling interval.
Which of the above is true?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Systematic sampling, a randomly chosen starting point is selected, followed by sampling at regular intervals (e.g., every kth element). So, S1 is correct.
• Systematic sampling can lead to biased results if there is a hidden pattern or cyclical nature in the population that aligns with the sampling interval. So, S2 is incorrect.

Q19 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Spanned strategy suitable for variable length records.
S2 : In the spanned strategy, a record can span multiple blocks.
Which of the above is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Spanned strategy a record can span across multiple blocks if the record size exceeds the block size.
• Spanned strategy suitable for variable length records because it allows records to be divided and stored across multiple blocks when they don’t fit in a single block.

Q31 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?
S1 : Ridge regression can be used to improve the performance of a linear regression model by adding a penalty term.
S2 : K-means clustering is used to fit a linear model to the data.
S3 : K-means clustering requires specifying the number of clusters before the algorithm starts.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Ridge regression adds a penalty term (L2 regularization) to the linear regression model to reduce overfitting and improve performance by shrinking the coefficients. So, S1 is correct.
• K-means clustering is a method for partitioning data into clusters based on similarity, not for fitting a linear model. So, S2 is incorrect.
• K-means clustering requires the number of clusters (k) to be specified before running the algorithm. So, S3 is correct.

Q32 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1  : Alpha-beta pruning is applied after evaluating every node in the Mini-Max tree.
S2 : The Mini-Max algorithm with alpha-beta pruning can be significantly faster than the standard Mini-Max algorithm due to reduced branching.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Alpha-beta pruning is applied during the evaluation of nodes, not after evaluating every node. So, S1 is incorrect.
• Alpha-beta pruning can make the Mini-Max algorithm significantly faster by reducing the number of nodes evaluated through pruning. So, S2 is correct.

Q38 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, B
Solution

(a, b)
• SVMs use kernel functions to map data into higher dimensions to find a separating hyperplane
when data is not linearly separable. So, option (a) is correct.
• LOO cross-validation uses each sample as a test set and the rest as the training set, repeating
this process for each sample. So, option (b) is correct.
• SVM is used for classification and regression, not for dimensionality reduction. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• LOO cross-validation is more computationally intensive than k-fold cross-validation because it requires training the model n times (for each sample). So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q43 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, B, C, D
Solution

(a)
• LDA aims to reduce dimensionality while maximizing the separation between classes. It projects data onto a lower-dimensional space in a way that enhances class separability. So, option (a) is correct.
• Ridge regression is a technique used to handle multi-collinearity by adding a penalty term to the regression coefficients, but it does not reduce dimensionality. It addresses overfitting by shrinking the coefficients. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• LDA can be used for both binary and multiclass classification problems. It’s not limited to binary classification. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Ridge regression does not involve transforming data into principal components. It adds a penalty to the regression coefficients to reduce overfitting, whereas Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is used for dimensionality reduction by transforming data into principal components. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q44 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Rejection sampling involves accepting samples from a proposal distribution based on their likelihood of matching the target distribution.
S2 : Gibbs sampling is an MCMC method that samples from conditional distributions iteratively to approximate a multivariate distribution.
S3 : The Gini index quantifies distribution inequality by comparing the area between the Lorenz curve and the line of equality.
Which of the following statements is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Rejection sampling uses a likelihood ratio to decide whether to accept or reject samples, not just accepting based on likelihood alone. So, S1 is incorrect.
• Gibbs sampling is indeed an MCMC method for generating samples by iterating through conditional distributions. So, S2 is correct.
• The Gini index measures inequality by comparing the area between the Lorenz curve and the equality line. So, S3 is correct.

Q49 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are true?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(a)
• R-squared indicates the proportion of variance in the dependent variable explained by the model. So, option (a) is correct.
• Decision Trees can use various criteria for splitting nodes, not limited to mean squared error.
So, option (b) is incorrect.
• K-means clustering is not hierarchical; it partitions data into k clusters. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• SVMs are primarily used for classification and regression, not clustering. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q50 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) Cluster sampling is preferred over simple random sampling when the population is heterogeneous within clusters.
(b) Clusters should be as homogenous as possible to minimize sampling error.
(c) Cluster sampling provides more precision than stratified sampling.
(d) In cluster sampling, smaller clusters generally lead to more accurate results.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: B, A, C, D
Solution

(b)
• Cluster sampling is preferred when there is heterogeneity between clusters and homogeneity within clusters. This ensures that the sample is representative. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• Clusters as homogeneous as possible reduces the variability within clusters, which helps minimize sampling error. So, option (b) is correct.
• Cluster sampling typically provides less precision than stratified sampling, especially when there is high within-cluster similarity. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Size of clusters alone does not guarantee more accurate results; it’s the representativeness and structure of the clusters that matter more. Smaller clusters do not necessarily lead to better accuracy. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q65 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) In two-stage cluster sampling, the second stage always involves selecting all elements from each selected cluster.
(b) Cluster sampling can be combined with stratified sampling to improve accuracy.
(c) Cluster sampling is ideal when the population consists of geographically dispersed group.
(d) Cluster sampling tends to give smaller standard errors compared to simple random sampling.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, C
Solution

(b, c)
(a) In two-stage cluster sampling, not all elements in selected clusters are always sampled; often, only a subset is chosen. So, option (a) is incorrect.
(b) Cluster sampling can be combined with stratified sampling to improve accuracy by ensuring that different strata are well-represented within clusters. So, option (b) is correct.
(c) Cluster sampling is ideal for geographically dispersed populations because it reduces logistical challenges and costs. So, option (c) is correct.
(d) Cluster sampling generally results in larger standard errors compared to simple random sampling due to higher within-cluster similarity. So, option (d) is incorrect.
